title: eucalyptus globulus subsp. maidenii
intro: Eucalyptus globulus subsp. maidenii, commonly known as Manna Gum, is a subspecies of the Tasmanian Blue Gum tree, native to Australia. It is a tall, evergreen tree with a broad, rounded crown, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and decongestant properties. The leaves of the Manna Gum tree have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including colds, coughs, sore throats, and bronchitis, due to their ability to relieve congestion and reduce inflammation. Eucalyptus globulus subsp. maidenii can be used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to relieve muscle and joint pain, or inhaled to ease respiratory issues.
